---
title: "Getting started with socialinfrascorepy"
---

## Overview

**socialinfrascorepy** lets you use the Social Infrastructure Scorecard from
Python. If you use the
[Social Infrastructure Scorecard Dashboard](https://github.com/timothyfraser/scorecard),
this package gives you programmatic access to the same data: view social
infrastructure sites (parks, community spaces, places of worship, and more),
request scorecards for new areas, and download results for analysis.

You need two environment variables: `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`.
These are provided when you sign up for access.

## Installation

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/timothyfraser/scorecard.git#subdirectory=socialinfrascorepy
```

## Step 1 --- Create a client

The `client()` function builds a lightweight configuration object that every
other function in the package requires.

```python
import os
import socialinfrascorepy as si

cli = si.client(
    os.environ["SUPABASE_URL"],
    os.environ["SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"],
)
```

## Step 2 --- Authenticate

Most operations require an authenticated session.  Use `sign_up()` once to
register, then `sign_in()` for subsequent sessions.

```python
# First time only
result = si.sign_up(cli, "you@example.com", "strong-password", name="Your Name")

# Subsequent sessions
auth = si.sign_in(cli, "you@example.com", "strong-password")
authed = auth["client"]
```

The returned `authed` client carries your session tokens and is passed to all
downstream functions.

## Step 3 --- Look up a neighborhood

You can find neighborhoods by OpenStreetMap ID, internal location ID, area ID,
or free-text place name.

```python
# By OSM ID
poly = si.get_boundary_by_osm_id(authed, osm_id=5128581)

# By place name search
candidates = si.search_locations(authed, query="Ithaca", state="NY")
boundary = si.get_boundary_by_place_name(authed, query="Ithaca", state="NY")
```

Example output (sample):

```text
   location_id   osm_id place_name    county state country  area_km2 geom_type
0        84217  5128581     Ithaca  Tompkins    NY      US     15.41   Polygon
```

## Step 4 --- Browse themes and keywords

Social infrastructure is organised into themes (e.g. community spaces, parks,
places of worship).  Browse what is available before submitting a request.

```python
themes = si.get_themes(authed)
keywords = si.get_theme_keywords(authed, theme_ids=[1, 3, 4])
```

## Step 5 --- Submit a scorecard request

Supply a GeoJSON polygon and, optionally, theme selections.  The server
validates geometry, checks your quota, and starts asynchronous processing.

```python
import time

geom = {
    "type": "Polygon",
    "coordinates": [[
        [-122.32, 47.60], [-122.30, 47.60],
        [-122.30, 47.62], [-122.32, 47.62],
        [-122.32, 47.60],
    ]],
}

result = si.submit_request(
    client=authed,
    geometry=geom,
    theme_ids=[1, 3, 4],
    sites_grid_sqkm=2,
)
```

## Step 6 --- Poll for completion and retrieve results

Processing takes a few minutes.  Poll the request status and retrieve the
scorecard when ready.

```python
time.sleep(30)
status = si.get_request_status(authed, result["request"]["id"])

if status["status"].iloc[0] == "success":
    scores = si.get_scorecard(authed, location_id=result["location_id"])
    sites = si.get_sites(authed, location_id=result["location_id"])
```

## Step 7 --- Check your account

```python
si.get_subscription(authed)
si.get_usage(authed, start_date="2025-01-01")
si.get_remaining_queries(authed)
si.get_requests(authed)
```

## Next steps

- Read the [Client API](client-api.qmd) article for a complete function
  reference with parameter details.
- See [Workflows](workflows.qmd) for an in-depth look at how requests are
  processed on the backend.
